--- 
title: "LandRCBM Manual"
subtitle: "`r paste('v.', Sys.getenv('SpadesCBM_MAN_VERSION'))`"
author: "John Doe"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
site: bookdown::bookdown_site
documentclass: book
bibliography:
  - citations/references.bib
csl: citations/ecology-letters.csl
biblatexoptions: [refsegment=chapter]
link-citations: true
---

# Overview  {-}

:::{.rmdimportant}
This documentation is work in progress. Potential discrepancies and omissions may exist for the time being. If you find any, contact us [here](https://github.com/PredictiveEcology/LandRCBM/issues).
:::

[LandRCBM](https://github.com/PredictiveEcology/LandRCBM) is a modular, transparent, and spatially explicit carbon modelling framework that links the [LandR](https://landr-manual.predictiveecology.org/) vegetation model with the [spadesCBM](https://predictiveecology.github.io/spadesCBM/#overview) carbon budget model. 

[LandRCBM](https://github.com/PredictiveEcology/LandRCBM) development follows the [PERFICT](https://predictiveecology.github.io/spadesCBM/lexicon.html#basic-structure-of-a-spades-project) approach of @mcintire2022 for ecological modelling systems, an approach that helps solve many of the complex issues in ecological modelling, supports continuous workflows, and nimble, enter operable modelling systems. The [SpaDES](https://SpaDES.PredictiveEcology.org) platform is the toolkit that enables this implementation of the [PERFICT](https://predictiveecology.github.io/spadesCBM/lexicon.html#basic-structure-of-a-spades-project) principle.

## Usage {-}
Three [modules](https://predictiveecology.github.io/LandRCBM/Lexicon.html#modules) of the [spadesCBM](https://github.com/PredictiveEcology/spadesCBM/tree/main) collection are used in [LandRCBM](https://github.com/PredictiveEcology/LandRCBM). These are [CBM_defaults](https://github.com/PredictiveEcology/CBM_defaults.git), which reads in default CBM parameters for Canada, [CBM_dataPrep](https://github.com/PredictiveEcology/CBM_dataPrep.git), the CBM data preparation module, and  [CBM_core](https://github.com/PredictiveEcology/CBM_core), the central module of [spadesCBM](https://github.com/PredictiveEcology/spadesCBM/tree/main) where carbon transfers between pools are applied at each time step. In-depth information on these modules can be found in the [spadesCBM manual](https://landr-manual.predictiveecology.org/index.html).

Five [modules](https://predictiveecology.github.io/LandRCBM/Lexicon.html#modules) of the [LandR](https://landr-manual.predictiveecology.org/) `Biomass_` series of modules are used to run the basics of [LandRCBM](https://github.com/PredictiveEcology/LandRCBM). These are [Biomass_borealDataPrep](https://github.com/PredictiveEcology/Biomass_borealDataPrep), a data preparation module for Western Canadian boreal forests, [Biomass_speciesFactorial](https://github.com/PredictiveEcology/Biomass_speciesFactorial), a data preparation module that runs factorial simulation experiments for LANDIS-II-like species traits, [Biomass_speciesParameters](https://github.com/PredictiveEcology/Biomass_speciesParameters), a calibration module for species growth and mortality, [Biomass_core](https://github.com/PredictiveEcology/Biomass_core), the primary forest succession module that simulates tree cohort aging, growth, mortality, light competition, and seed dispersal, and [Biomass_yieldTables](https://github.com/PredictiveEcology/Biomass_yieldTables), a module that generates yield tables based on [Biomass_core](https://github.com/PredictiveEcology/Biomass_core) outputs. While a part of the `Biomass_` collection of modules, [Biomass_yieldTables](https://github.com/PredictiveEcology/Biomass_yieldTables) was developed with LandRCBM in mind and has an associated chapter in this manual. More information on the other modules can be found in the [LandR manual](https://landr-manual.predictiveecology.org/index.html)

A [LandRCBM](https://github.com/PredictiveEcology/LandRCBM)-specific module was also created to bridge outputs between the two models and is necessary to run LandR simulations. [LandRCBM_split3pools](https://github.com/PredictiveEcology/LandRCBM_split3pools) builds yearly growth increments using the biomass outputs of [Biomass_core](https://github.com/PredictiveEcology/Biomass_core) and splits them between the 3 main aboveground biomass pools (merchantable, foliage, and other) for use in the carbon transfers of [CBM_core](https://github.com/PredictiveEcology/CBM_core). Details on this module's functioning can be found in its [manual chapter](https://predictiveecology.github.io/LandRCBM/landrcbm_split3pools.html). 

![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PredictiveEcology/LandRCBM/main/manual/images/LandRCBMconceptualDiagram.png){fig-align="center" width="650"}
*Figure 1: Conceptual diagram of a basic LandRCBM run with a disturbance module*

These modules are the basis with which one can run LandRCBM. However, thanks to the flexibility of the SpaDES platform, it is possible to link additional modules when running simulations. The most common use-case for this is the addition of one or more disturbance models. Fire models, for examples, can be run alongside LandRCBM and users can add the [Biomass_regeneration](https://github.com/PredictiveEcology/Biomass_regeneration) module to incorporation stand removal, serotiny, and regeneration into the vegetation dynamics.
